title: Trying to Sext My Partner, Who Replies “I Can’t Get My Camera to Work”
author: Kelli Russell Agodon
poem:
In this love at midlife, without

photos, without thighs or hipbones,

long moments where nothing happens—

> _oh sorry, just seeing this_

a dent in the couch

from too many movie nights, from years

of sitting in the same spot

> _I’m lost, did you send something_

and one less shadow, our kid

in college and their room empty

down the hall

> _maybe this isn’t our thing_

the quilt I bought for myself

covered in cat hair or an actual cat,

folded in half

> _I was getting the coffee ready_

so when I ask, _Do you still find me desirable?_

what’s missing are the three

little dots, the cue someone’s texting back.
